description: Phase 10 — PWA Installation. Generate icon asset set, expand manifest, add iOS meta tags and splash screens, and wire a custom Install app button using beforeinstallprompt.

userStories.title: Generate placeholder icon asset set
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a consistent set of app icons so the manifest can reference real image files.
acceptanceCriteria: @vite-pwa/assets-generator installed as a dev dependency via yarn add -D @vite-pwa/assets-generator in ui/

ui/public/logo.svg created — a simple placeholder: a blue rounded-square background (#1a73e8) with a stylised white wave silhouette centered, 512x512 viewBox, no text, no gradients, clean geometric shapes
ui/pwa-assets.config.ts created using createAppIconsOnlyPreset (or minimal2023Preset's app icons) from @vite-pwa/assets-generator/config with source image public/logo.svg
Script added to ui/package.json: 'generate-pwa-assets': 'pwa-assets-generator'
Running yarn generate-pwa-assets produces these files in ui/public/: pwa-192x192.png, pwa-512x512.png, pwa-maskable-192x192.png, pwa-maskable-512x512.png, favicon.ico, apple-touch-icon-180x180.png
Generated PNG files committed to the repo
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use a hand-written SVG for the logo — keep it under 50 lines, no gradients. The generator handles producing all PNG sizes plus maskable variants with safe-area padding automatically. Run the generator script from the ui/ directory.

userStories.title: Expand PWA manifest in vite.config.ts
userStories.description: As a user, I want the installed app to show a proper icon, name, and metadata so it feels like a native app.
acceptanceCriteria: ui/vite.config.ts manifest config expanded with: name 'surfsapp — Greek Surf Conditions', short_name 'surfsapp', description 'Surf conditions and forecasts for beaches across Greece. Offline-ready.', theme_color '#1a73e8', background_color '#ffffff', display 'standalone', orientation 'portrait', scope '/', start_url '/', categories ['sports', 'weather', 'travel'], lang 'en'

icons array added with four entries: { src: 'pwa-192x192.png', sizes: '192x192', type: 'image/png', purpose: 'any' }, { src: 'pwa-512x512.png', sizes: '512x512', type: 'image/png', purpose: 'any' }, { src: 'pwa-maskable-192x192.png', sizes: '192x192', type: 'image/png', purpose: 'maskable' }, { src: 'pwa-maskable-512x512.png', sizes: '512x512', type: 'image/png', purpose: 'maskable' }
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (open Chrome DevTools Application tab, confirm manifest loads without errors and all icon entries resolve)
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (icon PNGs must exist in public/). Service worker only works in production builds — run yarn build && yarn preview to verify the manifest.

userStories.title: Add iOS meta tags and favicon to index.html
userStories.description: As an iOS user, I want Add to Home Screen to use the correct icon and launch the app in standalone mode.
acceptanceCriteria: ui/index.html <head> updated with: <link rel='icon' href='/favicon.ico' sizes='48x48'>, <link rel='icon' href='/logo.svg' type='image/svg+xml'>, <link rel='apple-touch-icon' href='/apple-touch-icon-180x180.png'>

iOS-specific meta tags added: <meta name='apple-mobile-web-app-capable' content='yes'>, <meta name='apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style' content='default'>, <meta name='apple-mobile-web-app-title' content='surfsapp'>
Also added: <meta name='theme-color' content='#1a73e8'>, <meta name='description' content='Surf conditions and forecasts for beaches across Greece. Offline-ready.'>
Existing <title> and viewport meta tags preserved
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (inspect <head> and confirm all meta/link tags are present with correct values)
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (favicon.ico, apple-touch-icon-180x180.png, logo.svg must exist). These tags supplement the manifest — iOS Safari primarily reads apple-touch-icon and apple-mobile-web-app-* meta tags, not the manifest icons array.

userStories.title: Generate iOS splash screens and wire apple-touch-startup-image links
userStories.description: As an iOS user, I want a polished splash screen when launching the installed app so it feels native.
acceptanceCriteria: ui/pwa-assets.config.ts updated to use a preset that also generates iOS splash screens (e.g. minimal2023Preset or createAppIconsAndSplashScreensPreset with appropriate options)

Running yarn generate-pwa-assets produces iOS splash PNGs in ui/public/ covering at least: iPhone 14/15 (1170x2532), iPhone 14/15 Pro Max (1290x2796), iPad 10.9in (1640x2360), iPad Pro 12.9in (2048x2732) — portrait orientation

ui/index.html <head> updated with <link rel='apple-touch-startup-image' ...> entries for each generated splash screen, each with a matching media query for the target device (device-width, device-height, -webkit-device-pixel-ratio, orientation)
Generated splash PNG files committed to the repo
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill (open the built app in preview mode, confirm the splash PNG files exist at their public paths)
userStories.notes: The @vite-pwa/assets-generator output includes an HTML snippet for the splash <link> tags — copy it verbatim into index.html rather than hand-writing the media queries. Splash screens only activate when the app is launched from the iOS home screen in standalone mode.

userStories.title: Install prompt composable and Install app button in home header
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clear Install app button so I can add surfsapp to my home screen without hunting through the browser menu.
acceptanceCriteria: ui/src/composables/useInstallPrompt.ts created: exports canInstall (reactive boolean ref, true when a beforeinstallprompt event has been captured) and promptInstall (function that calls prompt() on the captured event, awaits userChoice, then clears the captured event)
Composable listens for beforeinstallprompt on window and calls preventDefault() to stash the event; listens for appinstalled event to clear canInstall
Composable cleans up event listeners via onScopeDispose
Composable also checks window.matchMedia('(display-mode: standalone)').matches — if true, canInstall stays false (app already installed)

HomeView.vue header gets a new icon button using <surf-icon name='download'> (fall back to 'plus' if download is not in the icon registry), styled consistently with the other header icon buttons (≥44x44px touch target), placed between the favourites toggle and the refresh button
Button is visible only when canInstall.value === true
Button has aria-label='Install app'
Clicking the button calls promptInstall()
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: beforeinstallprompt only fires in Chromium browsers (Chrome, Edge, Samsung Internet). Safari/iOS never fire it — the button simply won't appear there, which is expected. Test in Chrome DevTools Application tab with the 'Add to homescreen' link, or after meeting all install criteria in a production build.
